mei.relationship: Same mansion, same body. The site thinks like you do. Zero communication cost.
mei.daily_feel: Operational from day one, no cultural adjustment needed. But no surprises either.
mei.energy_flow_desc: Balanced both ways. Neither side drains the other.
mei.long_term_risk: Client starts questioning why they are paying for an external consultant who thinks the same way their internal team does.
mei.growth: Low. The site's challenges are familiar territory; nothing new to learn.
mei.stability: High. Stable during the contract, but perceived value declines over time.
mei.bad_year_impact: Bad year does not add extra drain from the site. Impact is contained.
mei.fit_for: Short-term advisory where rapid industry understanding is needed. Not suited for long-term residency.

hr.relationship: Consultant and site are on the same wavelength. Zero communication friction but lacking external perspective.
hr.daily_feel: Consultant blends in instantly, but the client may question the need for external resources.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year impact is minimal, but consultant's added value is also at its lowest.
hr.fit_for: Short-term capacity augmentation works. Not recommended as a long-term consulting arrangement.

gyo.relationship: Karmic pull from past lives. You feel an inexplicable sense of mission toward the site.
gyo.daily_feel: You unconsciously invest effort beyond the contract scope.
gyo.energy_flow_desc: Low-level output from you, cushioned by karmic momentum so it does not feel like waste.
gyo.long_term_risk: Gyo side treats the site's problems as their own, blurring the consultant boundary.
gyo.growth: Medium. Experience accumulates through fulfilling the site's mission.
gyo.stability: Medium-high. Mission-driven stability, but boundary blur is a risk with over-investment.
gyo.bad_year_impact: Bad year intensifies the mission drive. You may accept renewals you should not.
gyo.fit_for: Senior consultants with strong boundary awareness. Not suited for consultants early in their independent career.

hr.relationship: Consultant is mission-driven, investing beyond contract scope.
hr.daily_feel: Consultant is highly self-motivated. The site barely needs to manage them.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year may cause consultant burnout from over-investment.
hr.fit_for: Projects requiring high self-motivation. Boundary management is critical.

tai.relationship: The site naturally accepts you. Onboarding takes almost no time.
tai.daily_feel: Guided by the site, you reach operational speed quickly.
tai.energy_flow_desc: The site outputs a small amount of support. You are the receiver.
tai.long_term_risk: Over-reliance on the site's support system means you cannot take the methodology with you when you leave.
tai.growth: Medium. Passive growth following the site's pace; transferability to the next engagement is uncertain.
tai.stability: Medium-high. The site's support keeps you stable, but that stability is not yours to keep.
tai.bad_year_impact: Bad year is buffered by the site. Less impact than working independently.
tai.fit_for: Junior consultants pairing with seniors. Not suited for sites where you need to lead independently.

hr.relationship: Site naturally provides support to the consultant. Fast ramp-up.
hr.daily_feel: Consultant's adjustment period is short, but self-sufficiency may be lacking.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year is buffered by the site. Low risk.
hr.fit_for: Quick capacity augmentation. Do not expect independent perspective from this consultant.

ei.relationship: Your expertise improves the site, and the site's results build your track record.
ei.daily_feel: A positive feedback loop. Every delivery brings a sense of achievement.
ei.energy_flow_desc: You invest proactively. The site's positive feedback prevents you from feeling drained.
ei.long_term_risk: The loop feels too good to leave. You become a de facto employee without employee protections.
ei.growth: High. Mutual uplift. Site experience directly becomes a selling point for your next engagement.
ei.stability: Medium-high. Stable as long as the loop continues, but check contract protections.
ei.bad_year_impact: Bad year weakens the loop but does not reverse it into drain.
ei.fit_for: Medium to long-term resident consulting. Periodic contract review is essential.

hr.relationship: Consultant and site mutually invest, forming a positive feedback loop.
hr.daily_feel: Both sides gain. Collaboration quality continuously improves.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year weakens but does not reverse the loop. Manageable risk.
hr.fit_for: Medium to long-term projects needing stable output. High renewal value.

shin.relationship: The site proactively gives you resources and a stage. You are the beneficiary.
shin.daily_feel: The site manager trusts you, gives you latitude and support.
shin.energy_flow_desc: The site invests proactively. You are the receiver.
shin.long_term_risk: Over-dependence on a single site's support may weaken your ability to win engagements elsewhere.
shin.growth: High. The stage the site provides lets you build track record fast.
shin.stability: High. Shin is the most stable beneficiary position.
shin.bad_year_impact: Bad year may reduce site support, but it will not reverse into drain.
shin.fit_for: Consultants who need to build track record. Maintain other pipeline channels in parallel.

hr.relationship: Site invests resources in the consultant. Consultant is the beneficiary.
hr.daily_feel: Consultant thrives at the site. Client satisfaction is high.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year reduces support effectiveness but does not reverse it.
hr.fit_for: Sites willing to invest in the consulting relationship. High return on investment.

yu.relationship: You are the giver. The site draws energy and methodology from you.
yu.daily_feel: You actively output. The site receives but gives limited feedback.
yu.energy_flow_desc: You output continuously. The site absorbs. Your fatigue goes unnoticed.
yu.long_term_risk: Past six months, your tank runs dry. Reassess before any renewal.
yu.growth: Medium. Consulting methodology grows, but the drained portion needs off-site recharging.
yu.stability: Medium. First three months are stable. Fatigue builds after that.
yu.bad_year_impact: Bad year reduces your output capacity. Delivery quality may suffer.
yu.fit_for: 3-6 month short-term projects. Not suited for annual contracts.

hr.relationship: Consultant is the giver. Site gains energy but feedback is limited.
hr.daily_feel: High early productivity, but consultant fatigue is hard to detect.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year reduces consultant output. Delivery quality risk increases.
hr.fit_for: Short-term high-intensity engagement. Contract should not exceed six months.

sui.relationship: The site gives you resources, but you are on the Sui side: drain accumulates.
sui.daily_feel: The site is supportive, yet you feel increasingly exhausted.
sui.energy_flow_desc: Site invests heavily. Your ability to reciprocate diminishes over time. First month is the best; it declines from there.
sui.long_term_risk: By the time you want to exit, you are too drained to do a proper handoff.
sui.growth: High but passive. The site pushes you forward, but you do not choose the direction.
sui.stability: Medium-low. Comfort declines over time. Fatigue accumulates.
sui.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus Sui position equals double drain. Delivery quality may drop sharply during this period.
sui.fit_for: Short-term residency with a clear exit date. Contract must include a no-renewal option.

hr.relationship: Site invests resources, but consultant is on the Sui side with increasing drain.
hr.daily_feel: Site provides support, but consultant's endurance diminishes and output declines.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus high drain equals risk of early departure or delivery failure.
hr.fit_for: Short-term capacity fill works. Past three months, monitor consultant state closely.

an.relationship: You drive the site's stability, continuously outputting energy to keep the project running.
an.daily_feel: Like holding an umbrella nonstop. You keep things steady; the site depends on your stability.
an.energy_flow_desc: You continuously output to maintain stability. The site draws security from you.
an.long_term_risk: An side drains without realizing it. The more the site depends on you, the harder it is to leave.
an.growth: Medium. Project management skills grow, but you get locked into the stabilizer role.
an.stability: Appears high, actually draining. Stable while you are there; falls apart when you leave.
an.bad_year_impact: Bad year doubles the stabilization load. You may be depleted by the time the contract ends.
an.fit_for: Senior consultants who set firm boundaries. Service scope must be explicitly defined in the contract.

hr.relationship: Consultant drives stability but continuously outputs, draining without awareness.
hr.daily_feel: Consultant stabilizes the site, but long-term umbrella-holding carries depletion risk.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year doubles stabilization pressure. Consultant departure risk rises.
hr.fit_for: Sites needing a stabilizing force. Contract scope must be explicit to prevent unbounded expansion.

kai.relationship: The site continuously extracts energy from you. 'All else is unbearable.'
kai.daily_feel: First month feels energized. By month three, nothing you do feels enough.
kai.energy_flow_desc: The site absorbs your stability. Your energy flows out in one direction.
kai.long_term_risk: The consultant's advantage is being able to exit at contract end. But if you are already depleted, you cannot even manage a proper handoff.
kai.growth: Low. Energy is being extracted. No reserves left for growth.
kai.stability: Low. Kai is the most unstable position.
kai.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus Kai position may make this contract period the lowest point in your career.
kai.fit_for: Only if the contract is under two months and the rate is high enough. Otherwise, decline.

hr.relationship: Site continuously extracts energy from the consultant.
hr.daily_feel: Consultant's productivity visibly declines after month three.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus Kai position equals very high risk of early departure and delivery failure.
hr.fit_for: Extreme short-term sprint tasks only. Do not extend past two months.

ki.relationship: The site puts pressure on you that drives growth. A pressure-based dynamic.
ki.daily_feel: The site's challenges keep you on edge. Stimulating in the short term.
ki.energy_flow_desc: Site applies pressure. You absorb it. Drain is not trivial, but growth is the return.
ki.long_term_risk: Ki side has a pressure ceiling. Exceed it and growth turns into damage. Contract expiration is the natural safety valve.
ki.growth: High but at a cost. Pressure-driven growth. Schedule recovery time between engagements.
ki.stability: Medium. When pressure is calibrated, it is good stimulation. When it spirals, things break.
ki.bad_year_impact: Bad year may turn pressure from a driving force into a crushing one. Watch your health.
ki.fit_for: Stress-tolerant senior consultants. Schedule rest periods between engagements.

hr.relationship: Site applies pressure to drive consultant growth. Pressure-based interaction.
hr.daily_feel: Consultant has short-term drive, but excessive pressure impacts delivery.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year may crush the consultant rather than drive them. Backup plan needed.
hr.fit_for: Projects that need high-pressure results. Must be paired with appropriate rest mechanisms.

sei.relationship: You grow from the site's challenges. After adjustment, things keep getting better.
sei.daily_feel: Uncomfortable at first. Two weeks in, you realize your methodology got stronger.
sei.energy_flow_desc: The site's characteristics polish you. You grow passively. Drain is low.
sei.long_term_risk: Sei side has the lowest risk. The only issue is that initial adjustment may make the client doubt your competence.
sei.growth: High. Post-adjustment growth is solid and transferable to the next engagement.
sei.stability: High. Sei is one of the most stable positions after adjustment.
sei.bad_year_impact: Bad year makes the adjustment feel heavier, but no collapse.
sei.fit_for: Consultants who can tolerate initial discomfort. Post-adjustment output will exceed expectations.

hr.relationship: Consultant grows from the site's challenges. Performance steadily improves after adjustment.
hr.daily_feel: Consultant needs initial adjustment, but post-adjustment results exceed expectations.
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year increases adjustment discomfort but does not cause collapse. Low risk.
hr.fit_for: Sites that can accept initial consultant adjustment. Highest long-term return.
